title: The stuff Ritsuko was diving in in episode 1
display_title: The stuff Ritsuko was diving in in episode 1
wikitext:
[]: The stuff Ritsuko was diving in in episode 1             Quote Post by ChangCY5555 » Sat Nov 15, 2014 6:58 pm Hey, all. Quick question for reference: What exactly was Ritsuko doing when she was diving in Unit-01's cage in episode 1? And what's the weird pink liquid for (and what's it called)? Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 15, 2014 7:04 pm Coolant?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Nov 15, 2014 10:13 pm I always assumed it was LCL, but in truth, I imagine it could be any liquid. I figured it was there to help the Evas support their weight while in torpor.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Nov 16, 2014 12:34 am That was Yui's cage? I mean, that would make perfect sense, given the circumstances, but the editing and lack of visual resemblance aside from the... coolant (only now did I realize it's not the same as the bakelite) makes that seem unlikely. Honestly I always assumed it was a swimming pool. As for what that should suggest about Ritsuko's character, I too was more than a bit bewildered.

[]:             Quote Post by ChangCY5555 » Sun Nov 16, 2014 2:00 am I assumed that she was working on Unit-01, but in what capacity? Why'd she have to go diving herself? I be confused. o_O

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 16, 2014 3:19 am She's the expert, and is also privy to certain operational secrets about the Evangelions. Either reason could be used to justify the swimsuit introduction.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Nov 16, 2014 3:41 am She was diving in whatever coolant(?) fluid that the Evas rest in when they're not working. I'd take an educated guess and say that she was doing/observing Evangelion maintenance.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Sun Nov 16, 2014 4:37 am Or it could just be fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Sun Nov 16, 2014 5:06 am Rosenakahara wrote: Or it could just be fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Nov 16, 2014 6:49 am I think that whatever it is they flood the cage with has more to do with handling the weight of Eva during maintainence than anything else. Every time we seem then installing or repairing hardware, we see then being able to float the object to and from Eva, rather than having I get a ton of large, bulky, heavy cranes and lifting machenery in such a tight space. In that sense, it could just be liquid that would make the Eva rust. (Coolant makes me think that the Eva uses more servos than muscle to operate, so it doesn't come immediately to my mind as to what it might be.) Rosenakahara wrote: Or it could just be fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sun Nov 16, 2014 7:41 am ^ The weight relief is something I'm thinking, too. Without an active AT Field or power source, that's a lot of limp tissue just pulling down.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Nov 16, 2014 11:01 am The AT field gets them around the square cube law when they're powered up so they need fluid bouyancy when it's not. Presumably any time they're exposed to plain ol' gravity without the AT Field damages them and they need repair. I've always assumed they're immersed in LCL so they can absorb it to heal their biological parts.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Nov 16, 2014 11:17 am Chuckman wrote: I've always assumed they're immersed in LCL so they can absorb it to heal their biological parts.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Nov 16, 2014 12:01 pm I would assume LCL, but then if it were LCL then none I the scuba divers would need breathing apparatuses. I would honk that the slow leak from Lilith only provides enough LCL for pilots, so something else would be needed for weight relief.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sun Nov 16, 2014 12:04 pm The LCL in the plugs is specifically oxygenated for the Pilots, but LCL is not naturally oxygen enriched.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Nov 16, 2014 1:14 pm For what it's worth, the blood red fluid in Dogma in Q on which you have the islands of skulls is specifically labeled LCL in the various making-of materials. I think if it's fair to extend that back to Jo swimming it would be fair to extend that to NGE ep 1. So it's plausible they have the bulk of that for technical needs and the LCL used in the plugs is oxygen enriched as mentioned.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Nov 16, 2014 2:39 pm I don't think that stuff is LCL. The color isn't right.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sun Nov 16, 2014 2:48 pm Blood changes color depending on whether it's oxygenated or not. It wouldn't be much of a stretch to assume LCL can also change color as its chemical composition changes.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Nov 16, 2014 3:03 pm Yeah, but the stuff that we see flowing directly out of Lilith is orange as well. The stuff Eva-01 is sitting in is a reddish color. Both are naturally oxygenated via contact with the air (not enriched, though-- as Gob pointed out.)

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sun Nov 16, 2014 3:22 pm I was talking about change of chemical composition in general, not oxygenation in specific. Maybe the stuff the Evas stand in has other chemicals mixed into it, hence the change in color.
summary:
[]: The stuff Ritsuko was diving in in episode 1             Quote Post by ChangCY5555 » Sat Nov 15, 2014 6:58 pm Hey, all. Quick question for reference: What exactly was Ritsuko doing when she was diving in Unit-01's cage in episode 1? And what's the weird pink liquid for (and what's it called)? Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 15, 20
